content.problemStatement:
**The problem is nearly identical to Mex Tree (Max Version) except here you must find the minimum possible score**.

You have a tree with NNN vertices. A tree is a connected undirected acyclic graph on NNN vertices with N−1N - 1N−1 edges.

You want to assign **distinct labels** to the vertices of the tree. You will choose a permutation PPP of the integers [0,N−1][0, N - 1][0,N−1] and then assign the vertex XXX a label of PXP_XPX​.

Define MEX(S)MEX(S)MEX(S) for a set SSS, as the minimum non-negative integer not present in SSS. For example, MEX({1,0,2,2,4})=3MEX(\{1, 0, 2, 2, 4\}) = 3MEX({1,0,2,2,4})=3 and MEX({1})=0MEX(\{1\}) = 0MEX({1})=0.

Define f(u,v)f(u, v)f(u,v) as MEX({PX1,PX2,PX3,…,PXK})MEX(\{P_{X_1}, P_{X_2}, P_{X_3}, \ldots, P_{X_K}\})MEX({PX1​​,PX2​​,PX3​​,…,PXK​​}) where X1,X2,…,XKX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_KX1​,X2​,…,XK​ are the vertices on the unique simple path from uuu to vvv (including uuu and vvv themselves, so X1=uX_1 = uX1​=u, XK=vX_K = vXK​=v).

Define scorescorescore of the tree as ∑X=1N∑Y=XNf(X,Y)\sum_{X = 1}^{N} \sum_{Y = X}^{N} f(X, Y)∑X=1N​∑Y=XN​f(X,Y), i.e. the sum of fff over all pairs of vertices.

You must find the **minimum** possible value of scorescorescore if you can suitably choose the permutation PPP.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain 222 integers each, uuu and vvv - representing an edge in the tree.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **minimum possible score**.
constraints: The set of input edges define a tree
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200020002000.
